old_claim.note: Prospecting was treated as an enabling capability for precious-metal working without direct edge-level source support.

new_claim_absent: No direct prospecting dependency remains because the source-backed node represents known precious-metal working, not a separate search-and-discovery practice as a required prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and introduction: the review dates and contextualizes early gold and silver metallurgy but does not identify prospecting as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports a chronology and regional context for early gold and silver working.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Prospecting is discovery and resource-search context; retaining it direct would make a broad upstream activity part of the node's mechanism.

ontology_before: Gold and Silver Working depended directly on Prospecting, implying formal prospecting was required for the earliest scoped precious-metal work.
ontology_after: Gold and Silver Working keeps its source-backed chronology and no longer directly requires a separate prospecting node.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: prospecting is no longer a direct prerequisite for gold_and_silver_working.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to organized ore-discovery systems rather than precious-metal working.
A stronger source showed prospecting directly enabled the early Balkan and Aegean gold or silver evidence.
A later mining-specific precious-metal node replaced this native precious-metal-working scope.
short_reason: Prospecting is broad resource-search context, not the direct working mechanism.
